name: Phishing: Spearphishing Attachment
description: Adversaries send emails with a malicious attachment (e.g. a macro-enabled document) to gain execution on a victim system when the user opens it.

name: User Execution: Malicious File
description: Adversaries rely on a user opening a malicious file (document, executable, archive) to gain code execution.

name: Command and Scripting Interpreter: PowerShell
description: Adversaries abuse PowerShell to execute commands, download payloads, and run scripts, often in-memory to evade detection.

mitigations.name: Disable or Remove Feature or Program

name: Scheduled Task/Job: Scheduled Task
description: Adversaries create scheduled tasks to execute malicious code, establish persistence, or escalate privileges.

name: Boot or Logon Autostart Execution: Registry Run Keys / Startup Folder
description: Adversaries add programs to registry Run keys or the Startup folder so they execute automatically at boot or logon.

name: Valid Accounts
description: Adversaries use legitimate credentials to access systems, blend in, and evade detection.

name: OS Credential Dumping
description: Adversaries dump credentials (e.g. LSASS memory, SAM) to obtain account login and password material.

name: Process Injection
description: Adversaries inject code into legitimate processes to evade defenses and possibly elevate privileges.

name: Obfuscated Files or Information
description: Adversaries encrypt, encode, or otherwise obfuscate files and payloads to evade detection.

name: Application Layer Protocol: Web Protocols
description: Adversaries use HTTP/HTTPS for command and control, blending malicious traffic with normal web traffic.

name: Ingress Tool Transfer
description: Adversaries transfer tools or files from an external system into a compromised environment.

name: Exfiltration Over Alternative Protocol
description: Adversaries steal data over a different protocol than the C2 channel (e.g. HTTPS, DNS, FTP) to avoid detection.

name: Data Encrypted for Impact
description: Adversaries encrypt data on target systems (ransomware) to interrupt availability and extort victims.

name: Inhibit System Recovery
description: Adversaries delete or disable recovery features (e.g. volume shadow copies, backups) to prevent restoration after an attack.
